message.content:
Build the sidebar and session management components for the OmegA web app at /home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/.

This is a Next.js App Router app using CSS Modules (NOT Tailwind). The design system uses these CSS variables from globals.css:
--void: #02020c; --indigo: #6366f1; --violet: #8b5cf6; --lavender: #c4b5fd; --gold: #e4b84a; --white: #f1f5f9; --dim: rgba(241,245,249,0.45); --dimmer: rgba(241,245,249,0.18); --glass-bg: rgba(8,8,28,0.72); --glass-border: rgba(139,92,246,0.18);
Fonts: 'Orbitron' for headers, 'Space Grotesk' for body.

Read the existing files first:
- /home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/app/page.tsx (current monolith — understand existing session logic)
- /home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/app/page.module.css
- /home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/lib/api.ts
- /home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/app/api/sessions/route.ts

Create these files:

1. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` — Persistent left sidebar (280px desktop, overlay drawer on mobile <768px). Contains:
   - Omega symbol + "OmegA" branding at top (breathing animation)
   - "New Chat" button (prominent, glowing border)
   - Session list: scrollable, each session shows first message preview + relative time
   - Active session highlighted
   - Collapse/hamburger button for mobile
   - Glass background effect matching the dark cosmic theme

2. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.module.css` — All sidebar styles. Glass morphism, smooth slide transitions, scrollbar styling, hover states with subtle glow.

3. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Sidebar/SessionItem.tsx` — Individual session row component. Shows preview text, relative timestamp, active highlight with indigo glow.

4. `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Sidebar/SessionItem.module.css`

The Sidebar should accept these props:
- sessions: array of {id, preview, created_at}
- activeSessionId: string | null
- onNewChat: () => void
- onSelectSession: (id: string) => void
- isOpen: boolean (for mobile toggle)
- onToggle: () => void

Use 'use client' directive. Make it beautiful — this is the first thing users see. The vibe is futuristic command center, not boring SaaS. Subtle animations on hover, smooth transitions, the Omega breathes.

content.text: Now let me create the component directory and all four files.

All four files created:

- `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` -- Main sidebar component with branding (breathing Omega), new chat button with glowing border, session list, mobile hamburger/overlay/drawer pattern. Accepts the exact props spec you defined.

- `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.module.css` -- Glass morphism background, 280px fixed width, `translateX(-100%)` slide-in for mobile <768px, custom scrollbar, breathing keyframe animation, gradient glow on new chat hover, overlay backdrop blur.

- `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Sidebar/SessionItem.tsx` -- Individual session row with truncated preview (60 chars), relative time formatting (reuses the same logic from page.tsx), keyboard accessible, `aria-current` for active state.

- `/home/mega/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/components/Sidebar/SessionItem.module.css` -- Active state with indigo glow/box-shadow, hover gradient overlay via `::before` pseudo-element, lavender text highlight for active items.

Design notes: Uses all the CSS variables from globals.css (--void, --indigo, --violet, --lavender, --glass-bg, --glass-border, --dim, --dimmer). Orbitron for brand, Space Grotesk for body text. The Omega symbol breathes with a 4s ease-in-out opacity + text-shadow cycle. The session data shape `{id, preview, created_at}` matches what `/api/sessions` already returns.
